terms.definition: Single artificial neuron with weighted sum z = wᵀx + b and step/threshold activation producing a binary output. Canonical home for the one-unit linear threshold classifier in this roadmap.
Pre-activation z = w₁x₁ + … + w_d x_d + b — linear combination of inputs before activation. Canonical definition of the perceptron score in this topic.
Hard output rule ŷ = 1 if z ≥ 0 (or z ≥ θ), else 0. Contrasts with sigmoid (logistic regression) and smooth activations in deep nets.
Property that classes can be split by one hyperplane wᵀx + b = 0. A single perceptron can represent exactly the linearly separable Boolean functions and datasets.
On misclassification: w ← w + η(y − ŷ)x and b ← b + η(y − ŷ). Converges in finite steps when data is linearly separable.
Set {x : wᵀx + b = 0} where perceptron output flips between classes. In 2D this is a line; in d dimensions a (d−1)-flat.
Step size scaling weight and bias updates in the perceptron rule. Affects convergence speed but not separability feasibility.
